events.description: H.R. 9709 passed by the House with Section 2 providing no conveyance to person owning at least 640 acres within Crow Reservation
Senate Report No. 219 accompanied S. 2890, stating bill had approval of Indian Bureau and Indians
House Committee on Indian Affairs added restriction to Section 2: no sale to any person of more than 640 acres
Bill recommitted to House Committee on Indian Affairs
Section 2 changed to exempt grazing land from acreage limitation
Bill passed House with amended Section 2 exempting grazing land
Conference Report No. 1043 provided new Section 2 in lieu of amended paragraph
Department informed President that S. 2890 was returned with recommendation for approval
Act of June 4, 1920 (41 Stat. 751) signed by President, establishing acreage limitations on Crow land conveyances

Solicitor's Opinion D-48159 issued, holding that one owning not more than 640 acres agricultural land may acquire additional 640 acres, and one owning not more than 1,280 acres grazing land may acquire additional 640 acres
Commissioner of Indian Affairs issued instructions to stop all future sales unless compliance with acreage limitations assured, after learning of violations in late 1955
Commissioner of Indian Affairs suspended all sales of Crow lands in fall of 1955
Field Solicitor prepared opinion for Area Director construing Section 2 of 1920 Act regarding eligibility to purchase Crow lands
Bureau accepted Field Solicitor's views by letter
Crow allotment 1552 (80 acres) advertised for public sale
Crow Indian land sale at which Callie F. Kennedy bid on allotment 1552 (80 acres) owned by Beady Anderson Bad Boy
Commissioner's letter advising precautions in sale of restricted land on Crow Reservation regarding possible violations of Section 2
Field Solicitor prepared detailed memorandum on Section 2 procedure for prevention of evasive violation by dummy bidders
Field Solicitor prepared additional memorandum on Crow Act Section 2 matters
Department of Justice letter regarding resolution adopted by Crow Indian Tribal Council on prosecution of violators
Area Director transmitted file on bid of Callie F. Kennedy for 80 acres of Crow allotment 1552 to Commissioner for review
Crow Indian Tribal Council adopted resolution regarding prosecution of persons who may have violated Section 2 of 1920 Act
Commissioner of Indian Affairs memorandum with background information on Crow land violations furnished to Department of Justice
Assistant Secretary of Interior sent supplemental report to Chairman of Subcommittee on Indian Affairs on acreage limitation enforcement
Area Director Percy Melis informed of decision that Callie Kennedy sale could be approved if affidavit executed
Senator Murray forwarded letter from Beady Anderson Bad Boy to Commissioner of Indian Affairs regarding allotment 1552 sale

events.type: Letter from Beady Anderson Bad Boy
events.description: Beady Anderson Bad Boy wrote to Senator Murray seeking assistance regarding delayed decision on sale of allotment 1552
Assistant Secretary Roger Ernst responded to Senator Murray regarding Beady Anderson Bad Boy land sale matter

events.type: Solicitor letter to Department of Justice
events.description: Solicitor George Bebout transmitted Commissioner's memorandum to Malcolm Anderson, DOJ, requesting criminal or civil action on Crow land violations
Superintendent transmitted case assembly regarding proposed sale of allotment 3171 to Area Director
Area Director memorandum rejecting bid of Jo Annette Hammond for allotment 3171 as ineligible under Section 2 of 1920 Act
Area Director Melis sent memorandum to Commissioner enclosing rejection of Jo Annette Hammond bid and requesting review
Commissioner acknowledged Area Director's letter of May 1 regarding Jo Annette Hammond case and promised further reply
Solicitor issued memorandum to Commissioner on eligibility of landowners to purchase additional Crow lands, overruling Field Solicitor's opinion of May 23, 1957
Commissioner wrote to Area Director Melis with final determination on Jo Annette Hammond eligibility and legislative history review

events.type: Solicitor letter to Department of Justice
events.description: Solicitor transmitted report to Department of Justice recommending court action to set aside conveyances void under 1920 Act

events.type: Secretary letter to Crow Tribal Council Chairman
events.description: Assistant Secretary Roger Ernst responded to Edward P. Whiteman regarding conveyances of Crow Reservation lands in violation of acreage limitations
Billings Area Office prepared report presenting prima facie case against three landowners for violations of Section 2 of 1920 Act

financial_transactions.description: Bid by Callie F. Kennedy for 80 acres of Crow allotment 1552 at public land sale; money deposited at Crow Indian Agency but not paid out pending decision on 1920 Act compliance
Proposed sale of 320 acres of Crow allotment 3171 to Jo Annette Hammond; disapproved by Area Director due to acreage limitations of 1920 Act
Deposit of Jo Annette Hammond to be returned and land readvertised for public sale following disapproval of her bid

correspondence.subject: Conveyances of Crow Reservation lands in violation of acreage limitations
correspondence.action_requested: Explanation of status of enforcement actions
correspondence.outcome: Explained that Solicitor transmitted report to DOJ on April 6, 1960 recommending court action; sales since late 1955 in strict compliance

correspondence.subject: Court action to set aside conveyances void under Act of June 4, 1920
correspondence.action_requested: Institute court action to set aside conveyances and quiet title
correspondence.outcome: Submitted report with prima facie case against three landowners

correspondence.recipient_title: Assistant Attorney General, Criminal Division, Department of Justice
correspondence.subject: Crow Indian Tribal Council resolution regarding prosecution of violators of Section 2 of 1920 Act
correspondence.action_requested: Institute criminal or civil action to get definitive interpretation of Section 2 and lay basis for further litigation
correspondence.outcome: Enclosed Commissioner's memorandum of September 24, 1958 and supplemental report of March 11, 1958

correspondence.subject: Eligibility of landowners to purchase additional Crow lands as provided in Section 2, Act of June 4, 1920
correspondence.action_requested: Return proposed letter to Area Director; cannot concur with Field Solicitor opinion
correspondence.outcome: Field Solicitor opinion of May 23, 1957 overruled; Jo Annette Hammond ineligible for both classes of land

correspondence.subject: Sale of Beady Anderson Bad Boy land on Crow Reservation to Callie F. Kennedy
correspondence.outcome: Bureau concluded sale can be approved if Kennedy executes affidavit prescribed by Field Solicitor; Area Director informed September 18

correspondence.subject: Appeal for assistance regarding delayed decision on sale of allotment 1552
correspondence.action_requested: Find out when Solicitor will give decision about sale of allotment 1552
correspondence.outcome: Senator Murray forwarded letter to Commissioner of Indian Affairs

correspondence.subject: Beady Anderson Bad Boy, Allotment 1552, Crow Agency, Montana
correspondence.action_requested: Review and report on Beady Anderson Bad Boy land sale matter
correspondence.outcome: Referred to Bureau of Indian Affairs for consideration

correspondence.subject: Bid of Callie F. Kennedy for 80 acres of Crow allotment 1552
correspondence.action_requested: Review and advice on whether to approve sale given Kennedy's association with Faddis-Kennedy Cattle Company
correspondence.outcome: Commissioner concluded sale can proceed if Kennedy executes Field Solicitor's prescribed affidavit

correspondence.subject: Proposed land sale of Crow allotment 3171, Claude Mapes Bases to Jo Annette Hammond
correspondence.action_requested: Review and comments on memorandum rejecting Jo Annette Hammond bid
correspondence.outcome: Commissioner acknowledged and promised further reply; Solicitor later confirmed Hammond ineligible

correspondence.subject: Acreage limitation question in proposed sale of 320 acres of Crow allotment 3171 to Jo Annette Hammond
correspondence.outcome: Matter under consideration; files from National Archives ordered

correspondence.subject: Legislative history of Act of June 4, 1920 and Jo Annette Hammond eligibility
correspondence.action_requested: Advise Superintendent that Hammond ineligible for both agricultural and grazing land
correspondence.outcome: Confirmed disallowance of Hammond purchase; enclosed Solicitor opinions

correspondence.subject: Proposed land sale of Crow allotment 3171, Claude Mapes Bases to Jo Annette Hammond
correspondence.action_requested: Return case assembly unapproved; refund Hammond deposit; readvertise land
correspondence.outcome: Case assembly returned unapproved; deposit to be refunded

correspondence.sender_title: Assistant Area Director, Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Approval of award of bid Callie Kennedy for eighty acres Crow allotment 1552
correspondence.action_requested: Immediate decision needed so land may be readvertised if decision adverse to present purchaser

correspondence.subject: Bid of Callie F. Kennedy for 80 acres of Crow allotment 1552
correspondence.action_requested: Write to bidder explaining Bureau's concern; have her execute Field Solicitor's prescribed affidavit
correspondence.outcome: Case assembly returned; sale can proceed upon execution of affidavit

legislative_actions.outcome: Passed House with Section 2 providing no conveyance to person owning at least 640 acres within Crow Reservation
Senate Report No. 219 accompanied bill; stated bill had approval of Indian Bureau and Indians
Added to Section 2: no sale to any person of more than 640 acres of land
Bill recommitted to House Committee on Indian Affairs
Section 2 changed to exempt grazing land from acreage limitation
Passed House with amended Section 2 exempting grazing land
Signed by President as Act of June 4, 1920, 41 Stat. 751; Conference Report No. 1043 provided new Section 2
